meta.note: Central Asia + Caucasus, mostly Forum 18 / Asia-Plus sourced. Kazakhstan: court 'extremism' bans of Christian books and JW import refusals. Azerbaijan: police-list bans (Nursi collection, JW booklet). Uzbekistan: Ismailov's blacklisted novels. Tajikistan: the remaining titles from the Interior Ministry's 1 July 2015 list of 13 'Salafi' books (the others are already in the DB). Held: UZ Sahih al-Bukhari (blanket/low), Kyrgyzstan (court suit rejected), Turkmenistan (nothing title-specific). (Merged file: also retains the 12 entries from a parallel session's earlier version that this batch had overwritten.)

entries.title: Called to the Great Commission
entries.description_ban: Christian missionary book ruled 'extremist' by Esil District Court, Astana (15 Feb 2016), alleging it subverted state security and inter-confessional accord.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific court 'extremism' ban; documented by Forum 18.

entries.title: Healing the Broken Family of Abraham
entries.description_ban: Christian apologetic work on Muslim outreach, banned as 'extremist' by an Almaty court in November 2012.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific court 'extremism' ban; documented by Forum 18.

entries.title: Jesus: More than a Prophet
entries.description_ban: Collection of Muslim-convert testimonies (ed. Richard Wootton); banned 'extremist' by an Akmola Region court in November 2014.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific court 'extremism' ban; documented by Forum 18.

entries.title: What Does the Bible Really Teach?
entries.description_ban: Import rejected by the Religious Affairs Committee (Aug 2015); rejection upheld by the Astana Specialised Interdistrict Economic Court, 29 Feb 2016.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific import ban; documented by Forum 18.

entries.title: Keep Yourselves in God's Love
entries.description_ban: Import rejected alongside 'What Does the Bible Really Teach?'; upheld by the same Astana court on 29 Feb 2016.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific import ban; documented by Forum 18.

entries.title: Awakening from Sleep
entries.description_ban: Short Tabligh-Jamaat-linked book banned 'extremist' by a Karaganda court (in force 11 Jun 2019), on the grounds of the author's link to a banned movement rather than the content.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific court 'extremism' ban; documented by Forum 18.

entries.title: Risale-i Nur
entries.description_ban: Said Nursi's 14-volume Islamic theology collection appears on Azerbaijan's (police-compiled, State-Committee-sourced) banned-literature list; copies have been repeatedly confiscated as 'illegal' in raids.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific listing/confiscation; documented by Forum 18 (medium confidence).

entries.title: Who Are Doing Jehovah's Will Today?
entries.description_ban: Azeri-language Jehovah's Witness booklet on Azerbaijan's banned-literature list.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific listing; documented by Forum 18 (medium confidence).

entries.title: The Devils' Dance
entries.description_ban: Acclaimed Uzbek novel (Jinlar bazmi); Ismailov's works have been banned in Uzbekistan since a 1992 Karimov-era criminal case, and a clandestine print run circulated in 2016.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban within the author's standing blacklist; documented via Wikipedia (medium confidence).

entries.title: The Railway
entries.description_ban: Ismailov's earlier novel (Temir yo'l); banned in Uzbekistan with all his work and first published abroad under a pseudonym.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban within the author's standing blacklist; documented via Wikipedia (medium confidence).

entries.title: Al-Majmu'at al-'Aliyya
entries.description_ban: One of 13 'Salafi' titles added to Tajikistan's banned-literature list by the Interior Ministry (1 July 2015).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ministry ban; documented by Asia-Plus.

entries.title: Al-Juhud al-Hadithiyya
entries.description_ban: On Tajikistan's Interior Ministry list of 13 banned 'Salafi' books (1 July 2015).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ministry ban; documented by Asia-Plus.

entries.title: Sharhu Muqaddima fi Usuli-t-Tafsir
entries.description_ban: On Tajikistan's Interior Ministry list of 13 banned 'Salafi' books (1 July 2015).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ministry ban; documented by Asia-Plus.

entries.title: Favaidu mina-t-Tafsir
entries.description_ban: On Tajikistan's Interior Ministry list of 13 banned 'Salafi' books (1 July 2015).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ministry ban; documented by Asia-Plus.

entries.title: Taysiru-l-Ilah
entries.description_ban: On Tajikistan's Interior Ministry list of 13 banned 'Salafi' books (1 July 2015).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ministry ban; documented by Asia-Plus.

entries.title: Favaqir al-Izab
entries.description_ban: On Tajikistan's Interior Ministry list of 13 banned 'Salafi' books (1 July 2015).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ministry ban; documented by Asia-Plus.

entries.title: al-Ilaqat bayna-t-Tashayyu wa-t-Tasawwuf
entries.description_ban: On Tajikistan's Interior Ministry list of 13 banned 'Salafi' books (1 July 2015).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ministry ban; documented by Asia-Plus.

entries.title: Sharh Fadl al-Islam
entries.description_ban: On Tajikistan's Interior Ministry list of 13 banned 'Salafi' books (1 July 2015).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ministry ban; documented by Asia-Plus.

entries.title: al-Ali al-Bahiyya
entries.description_ban: On Tajikistan's Interior Ministry list of 13 banned 'Salafi' books (1 July 2015).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ministry ban; documented by Asia-Plus.

entries.title: Fadlu Ilm al-Salaf
entries.description_ban: On Tajikistan's Interior Ministry list of 13 banned 'Salafi' books (1 July 2015).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ministry ban; documented by Asia-Plus.

entries.title: Stone Dreams
entries.description_ban: Novella sympathetic to Armenians (Daş yuxular); copies were publicly burned, it was withdrawn from school curricula, and the author was stripped of his state honours and pension by presidential decree.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific suppression; documented by PEN International.

entries.title: A Fantastical Traffic Jam
entries.description_ban: Third novella of Aylisli's 'Farewell, Aylis' trilogy, satirising an oil-rich dictatorship resembling the Aliyev regime; suppressed and never publicly released in Azeri or Russian.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific suppression; reported by Eurasianet (medium confidence).

entries.title: Selected Hadith
entries.description_ban: Banned as 'extremist' by Alakol District Court (2 Aug 2016) over its association with the proscribed Tabligh Jamaat, though experts found no incitement.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific court ban; documented by Forum 18.

entries.title: Worthy Answers
entries.description_ban: Protestant title banned as 'extremist' by an Astana court (15 Feb 2016); the prosecutor could not say what harm it caused.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific court ban; documented by Forum 18.

entries.title: Share Your Faith with a Muslim
entries.description_ban: Brethren missionary's book banned as 'extremist' by an Astana court (15 Feb 2016) in the same ruling.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific court ban; documented by Forum 18.

entries.title: The History of Christianity in Central Asia and Kazakhstan
entries.description_ban: Protestant history banned as 'extremist' by an Astana court (15 Feb 2016) in the same suit.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific court ban; documented by Forum 18.

entries.title: Bu Kunlar
entries.description_ban: Book ('These Days') criticising post-independence Uzbekistan and the late Karimov; it was the basis for an extremism charge when the exiled author returned and was detained in 2017.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific political ban; reported by Eurasianet.

entries.title: Ventsenosny Skitalets
entries.description_ban: Historical novel ('The Crowned Wanderer') on the 16th-century Mughal general Bayram Khan; President Niyazov denounced it as 'historically inaccurate' and banned publication, and Esenov was arrested in 2004 for smuggling in copies.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific presidential ban; documented by PEN America and RFE/RL.

entries.title: Events of My Life
entries.description_ban: Businessman's memoir (Voqeahoi zindagii man); copies were seized from bookstores nationwide in August 2023 and the author imprisoned on 'incitement of enmity' charges over passages on southern Tajikistan's elites.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific nationwide seizure; documented by Lossi 36 and RFE/RL.

entries.title: Sharhu Lam'atu-l-Etiqad
entries.description_ban: Salafi creed commentary; one of 13 titles on the Tajik Interior Ministry's 2015 banned-literature list, prohibited from import and distribution following the 2009 Supreme Court ban on Salafiyya.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Named on the 2015 Tajik Interior Ministry Salafi-literature ban list; documented by Asia-Plus.

entries.title: Al-Usul min Ilm al-Usul
entries.description_ban: Classical Salafi work on the principles of jurisprudence; title #13 on Tajikistan's 2015 Interior Ministry banned Salafi-literature list, prohibited from import and distribution.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Named on the 2015 Tajik Salafi-literature ban list; documented by Asia-Plus.

entries.title: Kitab al-Iman
entries.description_ban: Classical Islamic work on faith; title #9 on Tajikistan's 2015 Interior Ministry banned Salafi-literature list, prohibited from import and distribution.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Named on the 2015 Tajik Salafi-literature ban list; documented by Asia-Plus.
